response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack and web developer with 15+ years of broad experience, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The most critical disqualifier is a complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is a non-negotiable hard requirement. Beyond that, the candidate lacks every core technical competency the role demands: no embedded security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), no Layer 2/3 networking or switch knowledge, no Linux kernel development or modification experience, and only incidental exposure to C/C++. Their career has been overwhelmingly focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and web application development, which does not transfer to embedded networking firmware work.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts demonstrates broad adaptability
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and brief embedded/hardware work at Altom Consulting shows non-zero low-level foundation
Strong English communication skills with public open-source portfolio and conference speaking experience
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
No experience with any required security technologies: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication, or encryption protocols
No Linux kernel development, source-code modification, or embedded networking/switch firmware background

key_factors.factor: No embedded security or networking domain expertise
